findings.title: CORS wildcard allows any origin
findings.description: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * is returned on every response. Any browser origin can make cross-origin requests to the API. In production this must be restricted to known consumer domains.

findings.title: Private key stored without file permission hardening
findings.description: The Ed25519 private key is written to data/demo_ed25519_private_key.pem with NoEncryption(). os.makedirs does not set restrictive file permissions. If the data/ directory is world-readable the private key is immediately compromised. No runtime enforcement prevents use outside demo mode.

findings.title: Audit log has no immutability protection
findings.description: Audit events are inserted via plain INSERT with no database trigger, row-level policy, or constraint preventing subsequent UPDATE or DELETE. An attacker with database write access can silently alter or erase the audit trail. Entries are not hash-chained, so tampering is undetectable without an external reference.

findings.title: Self-approval enforcement not encapsulated in grant_requests module
findings.description: The guard preventing an operator from approving their own request lives in server.py, not in grant_requests.approve_grant_request(). Any future code path calling the module function directly bypasses the guard. Authorization invariants should be enforced at the data layer.

findings.title: Denial reason field has no length bound
findings.description: deny_grant_request() accepts an arbitrary reason string and stores it in the database without length validation. A caller can write multi-megabyte strings per denial event, causing unbounded database growth and memory pressure during reads.

findings.title: expire_old_requests() is never triggered
findings.description: expire_old_requests() is implemented in grant_requests.py but is never called. There is no background job, cron trigger, or request-time hook that invokes it. Stale grant requests remain indefinitely in the requested state and can be approved long after their intended window.

findings.title: GL-092 test_deny_failure_is_surfaced has copy-paste bug in mock assignment
findings.description: test_deny_failure_is_surfaced assigns self.audit_mod.append_event = self.audit_mod.append_event, a no-op that leaves the real function in place. The test does not verify that an audit failure exception actually surfaces from deny_grant_request(). Adjacent rollback tests are valid but this specific assertion is unexercised.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Audit log immutability
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: High-severity gap; compliance and forensic integrity depend on INSERT-only enforcement. The current schema allows silent deletion or alteration of audit events.

recommendedNextIssues.title: CORS origin hardening
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: High-severity; Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * is trivially exploitable in browser-based integrations. Restrict to a configurable allowlist.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Private key file permission enforcement
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: High-severity key management gap. Set 0o600 on the private key file at write time. One-line fix with significant security value.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Grant module self-approval guard and denial reason length validation
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Encapsulates two medium-severity gaps: moves self-approval enforcement into grant_requests.py and adds a length bound on denial reason strings.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Request expiry background trigger
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: expire_old_requests() is dead code without a trigger. Stale requests accumulate indefinitely and can be approved outside their intended window.
